---
title: "LRP86base: Dose-response (pooled dose slope) — letter-sounds comparator to LRP86"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP86base is the **pooled-dose companion** to [LRP86](../lrp86/) (letter-sound
knowledge $L$, $N = 32$): a single pooled dose slope $\beta_{\text{dose}}$ instead
of partial-pooled per-period slopes, identical otherwise. Two uses: the nested
**PSIS-LOO** test of whether the $L$ dose–gain slope varies by period (almost
certainly not, as for word reading), and the **credible pooled dose-slope
estimate** — the headline "does dose help letter sounds?" number, since the
period-varying model's overall slope carries an inflated CI from its
partial-pooling hyper-prior. See [LRP86](../lrp86/) for the estimand, adjustment
set and expectations (Phase 1 implied a weak/null letter-sound dose effect).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Pooled dose slope

```{python}
# | echo: false
dose = pd.read_csv(_here / "dose_slope_summary.csv")
dose
```

`dose_pooled` is the single dose slope on the logit scale per 1 SD of per-period
dose; `p_pos` is $P(\text{slope} > 0)$. Compare against the word-reading pooled
slope in the LRP77base report.

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
